message:
Fix daemon socket ready race condition (bd-151)

Add WaitReady() channel to RPC server that signals when the socket is
listening and ready to accept connections. Previously daemon startup
waited a fixed 2 seconds which could fail if the server took longer.

Changes:
- Add readyChan to Server struct
- Signal ready after listener bind completes
- Update daemon startup to wait on WaitReady() channel
- Increase timeout to 5s with proper signaling

This fixes multi-repo daemon routing test failures where daemon would
start but not be ready to handle requests within the timeout window.

Amp-Thread-ID: https://ampcode.com/threads/T-675a2db5-b1b3-480d-a108-b003d8139d08
Co-authored-by: Amp <amp@ampcode.com>

files.patch:
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-130","title":"Add resource limits to daemon (connections, cache, memory)","description":"Daemon has no resource limits. Under heavy load or attack, it could:
- Accept unlimited connections
- Cache unlimited databases
- Use unbounded memory
- Exhaust file descriptors

Need limits for:
- Max concurrent RPC connections (default: 100)
- Max storage cache size (default: 50)
- Request timeout enforcement (default: 30s)
- Memory pressure detection

Location: internal/rpc/server.go","design":"Add resource tracking to Server:

type Server struct {
    // ... existing
    maxConns      int32
    activeConns   int32  // atomic
    connSemaphore chan struct{}
}

Use semaphore pattern for connection limiting:
- Acquire token before handling connection
- Release on completion
- Reject connections when full

Add configurable limits via env vars:
- BEADS_DAEMON_MAX_CONNS (default: 100)
- BEADS_DAEMON_MAX_CACHE_SIZE (default: 50)
- BEADS_DAEMON_REQUEST_TIMEOUT (default: 30s)

Add memory pressure detection:
- Monitor runtime.MemStats
- Trigger cache eviction at threshold
- Log warnings at high memory use","acceptance_criteria":"- Connection limit enforced
- Excess connections rejected gracefully
- Request timeouts work
- Memory limits configurable
- Metrics expose current usage
- Tests for limit enforcement
- Documentation on tuning limits","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:09.810963-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.292784-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-19T13:21:47.891925-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-131","title":"Add telemetry and observability to daemon","description":"Daemon has no metrics or observability. Cannot monitor:
- Request latency (p50, p95, p99)
- Cache hit/miss rates
- Active connections
- Error rates
- Resource usage over time

Needed for:
- Performance debugging
- Capacity planning
- Production monitoring
- SLA tracking

Location: internal/rpc/server.go","design":"Add metrics collection to daemon:

1. Request metrics:
   - Total requests by operation
   - Latency histogram
   - Error count by type

2. Cache metrics:
   - Hit/miss ratio
   - Eviction count
   - Current size

3. Connection metrics:
   - Active connections
   - Total connections
   - Rejected connections

4. Resource metrics:
   - Memory usage
   - Goroutine count
   - File descriptor count

Add metrics endpoint:
- bd daemon --metrics (JSON output)
- OpMetrics RPC operation
- Prometheus-compatible format option

Add to health check response for free monitoring.","acceptance_criteria":"- Metrics collected for key operations
- bd daemon --metrics command works
- Metrics include timestamps
- Latency percentiles calculated
- Zero performance overhead
- Documentation on metrics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:19.835495-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.292947-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-19T14:58:41.155435-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-132","title":"Add log rotation for daemon.log","description":"daemon.log grows forever without rotation. With sync every 5 minutes:
- ~105k log entries per year
- No size limit
- No cleanup
- Eventually fills disk

Need automatic log rotation with:
- Size-based rotation (default: 10MB)
- Age-based cleanup (default: 7 days)
- Compression of old logs
- Configurable retention

Location: cmd/bd/daemon.go:455","design":"Use lumberjack library for rotation:

import "gopkg.in/natefinch/lumberjack.v2"

logF := &lumberjack.Logger{
    Filename:   logPath,
    MaxSize:    10,  // MB
    MaxBackups: 3,
    MaxAge:     7,   // days
    Compress:   true,
}

Make configurable via env vars:
- BEADS_DAEMON_LOG_MAX_SIZE (default: 10MB)
- BEADS_DAEMON_LOG_MAX_BACKUPS (default: 3)
- BEADS_DAEMON_LOG_MAX_AGE (default: 7 days)

Add to daemon status output:
- Current log size
- Number of archived logs
- Oldest log timestamp","acceptance_criteria":"- Log rotation works automatically
- Old logs are compressed
- Retention policy enforced
- Configuration via env vars works
- Log size stays bounded
- No log data loss during rotation
- Documentation updated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:30.94896-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.293129-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T16:27:51.349037-07:00"}
-{"id":"bd-133","title":"Daemon production readiness","description":"Make beads daemon production-ready for long-running use, multi-repo deployments, and resilient operation.

Current state: Good foundation, works well for development
Target state: Production-ready for individual developers and small teams

Gap areas:
1. Resource management (cache eviction, limits)
2. Health monitoring and crash recovery
3. Process lifecycle management
4. User experience (visibility, feedback)
5. Operational concerns (logging, metrics)

Success criteria:
- Can run for weeks without restart
- Handles 50+ repositories efficiently
- Recovers from crashes automatically
- Users understand daemon status
- Observable and debuggable","acceptance_criteria":"All child issues completed:
- P0 issues: Storage cache, health checks, crash recovery, MCP cleanup
- P1 issues: Global auto-start, visibility, version checks
- P2 issues: Resource limits, telemetry, log rotation

- Confirm resource usage is bounded

 {"id":"bd-134","title":"Refactor import logic to eliminate duplication between manual and auto-import","description":"The import logic is duplicated in two places:
1. cmd/bd/import.go (manual 'bd import' command)
2. cmd/bd/main.go:autoImportIfNewer() (auto-import after git pull)

Both have nearly identical code for:
- Reading and parsing JSONL
- Type-asserting store to *sqlite.SQLiteStorage (where we just fixed a bug twice)
- Opening direct SQLite connection when using daemon mode
- Detecting collisions with sqlite.DetectCollisions()
- Scoring and remapping collisions
- Importing issues, dependencies, and labels

**Problems:**
- Bugs must be fixed in two places (we just did this for daemon mode)
- Features must be implemented twice
- Tests must cover both code paths
- Harder to maintain and keep in sync
- Higher risk of divergence over time

**Proposed solution:**
Extract a shared function that handles the core import logic:

```go
// importIssues handles the core import logic used by both manual and auto-import
func importIssues(ctx context.Context, dbPath string, store storage.Storage, 
                  issues []*types.Issue, opts ImportOptions) (*ImportResult, error) {
    // Handle SQLite store detection/creation for daemon mode
    // Detect collisions
    // Score and remap if needed
    // Import issues, dependencies, labels
    // Return result
}
```

Both import.go and autoImportIfNewer() would call this shared function with their specific options.

**Benefits:**
- Single source of truth for import logic
- Bugs fixed once
- Easier to test
- Easier to extend with new import features
- Less code overall","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-10-18T17:07:06.007026-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.293447-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T17:11:20.280214-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-135","title":"Complete auto-import refactoring to use shared importIssuesCore function","description":"The manual import command (bd import) was successfully refactored to use the shared importIssuesCore() function in import_shared.go, reducing code from 494 lines to 170 lines.

However, autoImportIfNewer() in cmd/bd/main.go still has ~298 lines of duplicated import logic that should use the same shared function.

**Current state:**
- ✅ Manual import uses importIssuesCore() (commit 790233f)
- ❌ Auto-import still has duplicated logic (lines 618-915 in main.go)

**Duplication includes:**
- SQLite store detection/creation for daemon mode (fixed in 790233f)
- Collision detection with sqlite.DetectCollisions()
- Scoring and remapping collisions
- Importing issues (update existing, create new)
- Importing dependencies
- Importing labels

**Benefits of completing this:**
- Remove ~200 more lines of duplicated code
- Ensure manual and auto-import have identical behavior
- Future bug fixes only need to be made once
- Easier to test and maintain

**Implementation:**
Replace lines 714-908 in autoImportIfNewer() with:
```go
opts := ImportOptions{
    ResolveCollisions: true,  // Auto-import always resolves
    DryRun:            false,
    SkipUpdate:        false,
    Strict:            false,
}
result, err := importIssuesCore(ctx, dbPath, store, allIssues, opts)
// Handle result and show remapping notification
```

Then update hash storage logic at the end.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-10-18T17:38:34.443872-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.293614-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T18:07:05.553928-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-136","title":"Add .gitignore to prevent noisy untracked beads files","description":"When using beads, git status shows several untracked files in .beads/ directory: .beads/.gitignore, .beads/db.sqlite, daemon.pid and daemon.lock files. These should be added to the project's .gitignore to prevent noise.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-10-18T18:27:16.424878-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.293771-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-19T09:05:48.4899-07:00"}
@@ -48,8 +48,25 @@
 {"id":"bd-141","title":"Add daemon RPC support for comments and label subcommands","description":"The 'bd comments' and 'bd label' subcommands don't work in direct mode because they don't inherit PersistentPreRun from root command. Need to add daemon RPC handlers similar to how show/update/create work.

Affected commands:
- bd comments <id>
- bd comments add <id> "text"
- bd label list <id>
- bd label add <id> <label>
- bd label remove <id> <label>

Solution: Add RPC handlers in daemon.go for these operations and update the CLI commands to use daemon RPC when available (check daemonClient != nil pattern used in other commands).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-19T16:08:42.16553-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.294565-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-142","title":"MCP server workspace routing broken - using wrong server for workspace","description":"When working in ~/src/beads, AI agent is calling mcp__beads-wyvern__* functions which are configured for ~/wyvern workspace. This causes MCP commands to fail or operate on wrong database.

Expected: Should use correct MCP server based on current workspace
Actual: Using beads-wyvern MCP server when in beads repo

Need to investigate:
- How MCP server routing/selection works
- Why wrong server is being selected
- How to fix workspace detection","notes":"Root cause: Using multiple MCP servers (beads-adar, beads-wyvern, beads-vc, beads) instead of single MCP server with global daemon. AI randomly selects wrong server for workspace.

Fixed:
1. Started global daemon: bd daemon --global
2. Simplified config to single MCP server in ~/.config/amp/settings.json
3. Updated AGENTS.md to emphasize single MCP server as RECOMMENDED approach
4. Marked legacy multiple-server approach with warning about workspace routing issues

User needs to restart Amp for config changes to take effect.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-19T18:32:04.513755-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.294718-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-19T18:35:00.167234-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-143","title":"Renumber command fails with foreign key constraint error","description":"When running 'bd renumber --force' after deleting issues, the command fails with: 'failed to rename bd-73 to temp ID: failed to update issue ID: constraint failed: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed (787)'. This suggests the renumber implementation doesn't properly handle foreign key constraints during the ID swap process. May need to disable foreign keys temporarily or use a different renumbering strategy.","notes":"Deeper investigation: All child tables (dependencies, labels, events, dirty_issues, issue_snapshots, compaction_snapshots, comments) have FK constraints to issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. When renumbering tries to UPDATE issues SET id = temp-uuid WHERE id = bd-32, the FK checks fire immediately despite PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF being called. Issue might be that Go sql.DB connection pooling means the PRAGMA isn't applied to the actual connection doing the UPDATE. Testing with explicit connection (s.db.Conn()) to ensure PRAGMA sticks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-19T19:33:20.324768-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:57.175627-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:57.175627-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-15","title":"Implement full cross-type cycle prevention in AddDependency","description":"Expand cycle prevention in AddDependency to check for cycles across ALL dependency types, not just 'blocks'. Currently only 'blocks' type dependencies are checked for cycles, allowing cross-type circular dependencies to form (e.g., A blocks B, B parent-child A). This can cause semantic confusion and is a maintenance hazard for future operations that traverse dependencies.","design":"Implementation approach:
1. Modify the cycle check in AddDependency (postgres.go:559-599)
2. Remove the 'type = blocks' filter from the recursive CTE
3. Check for cycles regardless of dependency type being added
4. Return a clear error message indicating which types form the cycle

Trade-offs to consider:
- This is more mathematically correct (no cycles in dependency DAG)
- May break legitimate use cases where cross-type cycles are intentional
- Need to evaluate whether ANY cross-type cycles are valid in practice
- Alternative: make this configurable with a --allow-cycle flag

Before implementing, should investigate:
- Are there legitimate reasons for cross-type cycles?
- What's the performance impact on large graphs (1000+ issues)?
- Should certain type combinations be allowed to cycle?","acceptance_criteria":"- AddDependency prevents cycles across all dependency types, not just 'blocks'
- Clear error message when cycle would be created, including dependency types
- All existing tests pass
- Performance benchmarked on large dependency graphs (100+ issues)
- Decision documented on whether to add --allow-cycle flag or exception rules","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.272629-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T20:31:19.174534-07:00"}

When running bd list in repo A after creating an issue, it shows 0 issues. Debug output shows 'daemon socket not ready after 5 seconds' despite daemon process running.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-19T20:01:18.977976-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T20:07:40.203797-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-19T20:07:40.203797-07:00"}

Indicates timing issue where process exits between check and kill.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-19T20:01:18.979589-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T20:01:18.979589-07:00"}

This violates DRY principle and makes maintenance harder. Refactor to use flushToJSONL() with a force parameter to bypass isDirty check, or extract shared logic into a helper function. Located in cmd/bd/main.go:104-138.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.2728-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:44:24.167574-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-19","title":"Verify auto-export works","description":"","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-19T19:35:42.274255-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-14T14:16:09.268591-07:00"}

@@ -816,13 +816,15 @@ func runDaemonLoop(interval time.Duration, autoCommit, autoPush bool, logPath, p
 			}
 		}()
 		
-		// Wait for server to start or fail
+		// Wait for server to be ready or fail
 		select {
 		case err := <-serverErrChan:
 			log("RPC server failed to start: %v", err)
 			os.Exit(1)
-		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
-			log("Global RPC server started")
+		case <-server.WaitReady():
+			log("Global RPC server ready (socket listening)")
+		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
+			log("WARNING: Server didn't signal ready after 5 seconds (may still be starting)")
 		}
 		
 		// Wait for shutdown signal
@@ -883,14 +885,15 @@ func runDaemonLoop(interval time.Duration, autoCommit, autoPush bool, logPath, p
 		}
 	}()
 	
-	// Wait for server to start or fail
+	// Wait for server to be ready or fail
 	select {
 	case err := <-serverErrChan:
 		log("RPC server failed to start: %v", err)
 		os.Exit(1)
-	case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
-		// If no error after 2 seconds, assume success
-		log("RPC server started")
+	case <-server.WaitReady():
+		log("RPC server ready (socket listening)")
+	case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
+		log("WARNING: Server didn't signal ready after 5 seconds (may still be starting)")
 	}
 
 	sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
